subject: 🏃 Monday — Week 1 kickoff: Lower A + Upper Push
html:
Welcome to Week 1 of 15. Today officially launches the 15-week build to SF Marathon on July 26. No running today — Lower A + Upper Push in the gym, ~55 min.

Today's session: Lower A (single-leg squat to bench, single-leg RDL, step-downs, Copenhagen plank, single-leg calf raises, wall sit finisher) + Upper Push (bench, overhead press, push-ups, lateral raises, triceps). Keep weight conservative this week — bodyweight or 10–15 lb dumbbells. We're establishing the pattern, not chasing PRs.

Focus cue: The single-leg squat to bench is the runner's knee exercise. Control a 3-second descent every rep — no dropping into the bench. If form breaks down, cut reps before adding weight.

Context: Knee was yellow after the 10.5 mi Cambria run (tingly/tender afterward). A full strength day as day one is well-timed — gives the knee 48+ hours to settle before your first easy 5 tomorrow. Watch how it feels during wall sits and step-downs; any sharp pain, back off immediately.

Heads up: Week 3 is the Foster City 5K (May 2) — that race will recalibrate your MP targets. Let's get there healthy. The knee is the boss.
